{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: {#exr-prac-linreg-score}
#### Score equations for simple linear regression
(adapted from @dobson4e, Chapter 6)

Using the log-likelihood from @exr-prac-linreg-lik,
derive the score equations for $\beta_0$ and $\beta_1$.
That is, compute
$\frac{\partial \ell}{\partial \beta_0} = 0$
and
$\frac{\partial \ell}{\partial \beta_1} = 0$,
and show they are equivalent to the normal equations:

$$
\ba
\sum_{i=1}^n y_i &= n\hat\beta_0 + \hat\beta_1 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i
\\
\sum_{i=1}^n x_i y_i &= \hat\beta_0 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i + \hat\beta_1 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2
\ea
$$
:::

::: {.solution}

From @exr-prac-linreg-lik:

$$
\ell = -\frac{n}{2}\logf{2\pi\sigma^2}
       - \frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)^2
$$

**Score with respect to $\beta_0$:**

$$
\ba
\deriv{\beta_0}\ell
&= -\frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\deriv{\beta_0}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)^2
\\
&= -\frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n 2(y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)(-1)
\\
&= \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)
\ea
$$

Setting equal to zero:

$$
\ba
0 &= \sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)
\\
\sum_{i=1}^n y_i &= n\beta_0 + \beta_1\sum_{i=1}^n x_i
\ea
$$

**Score with respect to $\beta_1$:**

$$
\ba
\deriv{\beta_1}\ell
&= \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i(y_i - \beta_0 - \beta_1 x_i)
\ea
$$

Setting equal to zero:

$$
\sum_{i=1}^n x_i y_i = \beta_0\sum_{i=1}^n x_i + \beta_1\sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2
$$

These are the **normal equations**,
whose solution gives the OLS/MLE estimators
$\hat\beta_0$ and $\hat\beta_1$.

:::
